values: Review three English renderings before any bilingual analytical dataset is generated.
Variant A — historically explicit analytical English
Historically explicit; preserves legal phase, sex distinctions, and source-era ambiguity.
Compact dashboard labels; easiest to chart, but loses some nuance.
Publication-aligned/literary wording, especially Brian Reeve terminology; less uniform for analytics.
This workbook maps compact categories and field labels. Names are transliterated, while settlements, origins, occupations, detailed status, comments, and notes require dedicated lookup/review stages.

Choose Variant A as the canonical English analytical vocabulary. Keep Variant C in documentation as a concordance to Reeve's English edition. Use Variant B only for compact chart labels. Never replace the Russian columns: add parallel *_en fields through lookup tables after the current quality check is frozen.

Searchable terminology includes Alexandrovsk District, Korsakovsk District, Tymovsk District, free-status, cohabitant, penal labour, penal servitude, peasants-in-exile, settled exiles.
Scholarly comparison identifies two full English translations: Luba & Michael Terpak (1967) and Brian Reeve (1993), and warns that historical realia cause translation errors.
